from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase fragment *without* repeating the subject.
    phrase_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda o: f"is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda o: f"weighs {o}",
        "club": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda o: f"formerly played for {o}",
        "position": lambda o: f"plays as a {o}",
        "league": lambda o: f"competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "ground": lambda o: f"home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda o: f"stadium is {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda o: f"was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda o: f"was the {o}th pick",
        "college": lambda o: f"attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda o: f"started at youth club {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda o: f"wears number {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda o: f"started his professional career in {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda o: f"debuted with {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda o: f"was drafted by {o}",
        # Default fallback
        "default": lambda p, o: f"{p} {o}",
    }

    # Group information by subject, keeping predicate and object for later merging.
    grouped = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subj, pred, obj = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        formatter = phrase_map.get(pred)
        if formatter:
            try:
                fragment = formatter(obj)
            except TypeError:
                fragment = phrase_map["default"](pred, obj)
        else:
            fragment = phrase_map["default"](pred, obj)

        # store a tuple so we can later know the original predicate and object
        grouped.setdefault(subj, []).append((pred, obj, fragment))

    # Merge subjects that are objects of another subject (e.g., club -> ground)
    merged = set()
    clauses = []
    for subj, items in list(grouped.items()):
        if subj in merged:
            continue

        # Group objects by predicate to handle multiple values gracefully
        pred_to_objs = {}
        for pred, obj, _ in items:
            pred_to_objs.setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

        built_fragments = []

        for pred, objs in pred_to_objs.items():
            # If the object itself is a subject with its own facts, embed them as a relative clause
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub", "currentteam"} and len(objs) == 1 and objs[0] in grouped:
                obj = objs[0]
                obj_items = grouped[obj]
                obj_phrases = [it[2] for it in obj_items]
                # Use the original fragment for the relation
                base_frag = phrase_map[pred](obj)
                rel_clause = f"{base_frag}, which {' and '.join(obj_phrases)}"
                built_fragments.append(rel_clause)
                merged.add(obj)
                continue

            # Choose appropriate wording for multiple objects of the same predicate
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub", "formerTeam", "currentteam", "draftTeam"}:
                if len(objs) == 1:
                    frag = phrase_map[pred](objs[0])
                else:
                    # List all clubs/teams with commas and "and"
                    club_list = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
                    verb = "has played for" if pred in {"club", "formerTeam", "draftTeam"} else "currently plays for"
                    frag = f"{verb} {club_list}"
                built_fragments.append(frag)
            elif pred == "youthclub":
                if len(objs) == 1:
                    frag = phrase_map[pred](objs[0])
                else:
                    yc_list = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
                    frag = f"started at youth clubs {yc_list}"
                built_fragments.append(frag)
            else:
                # For other predicates, concatenate multiple objects with "and"
                if len(objs) == 1:
                    frag = phrase_map.get(pred, lambda o: f"{pred} {o}")(objs[0])
                else:
                    obj_list = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
                    frag = f"{pred.replace('_', ' ')} {obj_list}"
                built_fragments.append(frag)

        # compose clause for this subject
        if not built_fragments:
            continue
        if len(built_fragments) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {built_fragments[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(built_fragments[:-1]) + f", and {built_fragments[-1]}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return f"{clauses[0]}."
    else:
        *first_clauses, last_clause = clauses
        sentence_body = ", ".join(first_clauses) + ", and " + last_clause
        return sentence_body + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
